Algeria.text: Algeria's group finale against Austria was a six-goal thriller that ended in the most dramatic fashion: Mahrez's equaliser in the third minute of stoppage time salvaged a 3-3 draw after Kalajdzic had seemingly stolen the points in the 90th, a nervy escape that summed up a group stage full of turbulence. That point was enough, just, to carry them through as one of the best third-placed sides. Switzerland await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT — a disciplined, well-organised European side who will fancy their chances against an Algeria team that has looked vulnerable in defence. Survive that, and Colombia or Ghana could follow in the Round of 16, with Argentina a looming possibility in the quarterfinals should Algeria find the consistency that has so far eluded them.

Argentina.text: Argentina closed out Group J in composed, controlled fashion — two goals before half-time against Jordan, a Messi finish ten minutes from time to seal a 3-1 win, and the concession only of a brief second-half equalizer that the champions absorbed without alarm. Cape Verde await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a tie Argentina enter as heavy favorites: the African side are plucky and well-organized but outgunned at this level. Should the holders progress as expected, Colombia or Switzerland could emerge as genuine tests at the quarterfinal stage.

Australia.text: Australia closed the group stage with a goalless draw against Paraguay — a functional, unadventurous result that secured second place without revealing much about their ceiling or their nerve. They head into the Round of 32 to face Egypt on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT in a tie that carries genuine uncertainty: the Socceroos are not overwhelming favourites, and Egypt possess enough quality and tournament experience to make this uncomfortable. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 on Tue Jul 7 09:00 PT — a far sterner examination of whether this side is merely present or genuinely dangerous.

Austria.text: Austria scraped through Group J on the back of a frantic 3-3 draw with Algeria — Kalajdzic's stoppage-time equaliser sparing their blushes after Mahrez had twice put the Algerians ahead in the final exchanges, a result that flattered neither side. Now they face Spain in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT, and few would back Ralf Rangnick's side to come through: Spain arrive as heavy favourites, and Austria's leaky defence — six goals conceded in the group — offers them precious little margin for error. Should they somehow conjure an upset, Croatia or Portugal await in the Round of 16, with Belgium a potential quarterfinal obstacle beyond that.

Belgium.text: Belgium closed the group stage in emphatic fashion, dismantling New Zealand 5-1 in a performance that brooked little argument — Trossard's brace and a late flurry from Lukaku and Saelemaekers turned a comfortable victory into a statement. They meet Senegal in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT, a tie in which the Red Devils carry the weight of favouritism but one that demands respect: Senegal are a physically formidable, well-organised side capable of punishing any complacency. Survive that, and the draw opens into genuinely serious territory — Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinals, with France a looming possibility beyond.

Bosnia & Herzegovina.text: Bosnia & Herzegovina closed out their group campaign with purpose, putting Qatar away 3-1 through two sharp first-half goals from Alajbegovic and Abunada before Mahmic sealed it late, a performance comfortable enough to earn their passage as one of the better third-placed finishers. Now comes the hard part: a Round of 32 date with host nation USA on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT, a fixture in which the Americans carry the weight of expectation and home crowd, making Bosnia decided underdogs with plenty to prove. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal await in the last sixteen — with the very real possibility of a quarterfinal against Portugal or Spain lurking beyond.

Brazil.text: Brazil survived a nervy afternoon against Japan, going behind to Sano's 29th-minute strike before Casemiro levelled and Martinelli rescued the win deep in stoppage time — a reminder that the five-time champions are through, but not yet convincing. Norway await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, a physically imposing side capable of making life uncomfortable, though the weight of reputation and Brazil's superior attacking depth still makes the Seleção clear favourites. Win that, and England or Mexico could emerge from the quarterfinal bracket, with Argentina lurking as a potential blockbuster semifinal on the other side of the draw.

Canada.text: Canada survived the Round of 32 on the thinnest of margins, Stephen Eustáquio's goal deep in stoppage time separating them from South Africa in a contest that offered little comfort until the very end. The hosts now face Morocco in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT — a genuinely stern examination, with the Atlas Lions among the tournament's more dangerous sides and Canada yet to demonstrate the authority their home support craves. Should they advance, France or Paraguay await in the quarterfinal, with the prospect of Spain, Portugal, or the United States looming beyond that.

Colombia.text: Colombia close out Group K with a composed, disciplined 0-0 against Portugal — a result that flatters neither side but confirms the Colombians' defensive solidity and their comfort operating at the tournament's upper register. They enter the knockout rounds as group winners, meeting Ghana in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a tie Colombia will be expected to manage with authority given their superior pedigree and the form they have shown across the group stage. A passage to the Round of 16 would likely bring Algeria or Switzerland — a stiffer but navigable examination — before the quarterfinal draw opens the possibility of Argentina, a fixture that would carry the full weight of South American rivalry.

Croatia.text: Croatia edged past Ghana in unconvincing fashion, leading through Sucic's first-half strike before a Luckassen equaliser threatened to unravel their group-stage exit — Vlasic's 83rd-minute winner rescuing the points and second place in Group L. That nervy finish is unlikely to inspire fear ahead of Thursday's Round of 32 meeting with Portugal (Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT), a genuinely stern examination: Martínez's side carry the weight of expectation, and Croatia's modest form makes this a daunting assignment rather than an open contest. Survive that, and Spain or Austria await in the Round of 16 — with France a looming possibility further down the draw.

Curaçao.text: Curaçao's group stage ended with a 0-2 defeat to Ivory Coast, Nicolas Pépé doing the damage with goals either side of half-time — the first arriving inside seven minutes to set a tone the Curaçao defence never recovered from. They finished fourth in Group E with a single point and a goal difference of minus nine, yet still advanced to the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides. There, their journey came to an abrupt end, the gulf in quality across the group stage ultimately a true reflection of where this Curaçao side stands on the world stage.

Czech Republic.text: Czech Republic's group stage ended with a thumping 3-0 defeat to Mexico, the goals arriving in a clinical second-half burst and a stoppage-time finish that underlined the gulf in class — a deflating exit for a side that managed just one point across three matches. Having finished fourth in Group A and crept through as one of the best third-placed teams, they were nonetheless unable to sustain that precarious foothold, and Mexico's composed, purposeful display exposed their limitations emphatically. The Czechs depart at the earliest opportunity, their tournament leaving little to build on.

DR Congo.text: DR Congo finished third in Group K but ended on a sharp note, coming from behind to beat Uzbekistan 3-1 after Shomurodov's early opener — two Wissa strikes, the second a stoppage-time penalty, and a Mayele finish in between giving the result a convincing final shape. That form counts for little in the Round of 32, where England await on Wed Jul 1 09:00 PT: a heavyweight assignment by any measure, with Gareth Southgate's side commanding strong favourites against a team that scraped through as one of the best third-placed finishers. Survive that, and Ecuador or Mexico could follow in the last 16, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Brazil or Norway raises the stakes further.

Ecuador.text: Ecuador closed the group stage with a result that will resonate — a 2-1 comeback win over Germany, surrendering an early Sané strike before Angulo levelled and Plata's second-half finish sealed a famous three points. That was enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, and now comes a Round of 32 date with Mexico on Tue Jun 30 18:00 PT — a continental rivalry with real edge, and one where neither side arrives with a decisive psychological advantage, making it as close to a coin-flip as the draw could have produced. Survive that, and England potentially await in the Round of 16, before the quarterfinal bracket throws up the prospect of Brazil.

Egypt.text: Egypt edged through Group G in scrappy fashion, their final outing against Iran a nervy, front-loaded affair — Saber's fifth-minute opener was cancelled out almost immediately, and the sides settled for a point apiece that did the job without inspiring confidence. Australia await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a winnable tie on paper but far from a formality — the Socceroos carry genuine knockout-round menace, and Egypt's inconsistent group showing makes this a genuine contest rather than a procession. Survive that, and Argentina could loom in the Round of 16 — a sterner examination than anything the Pharaohs have faced so far.

England.text: England closed out Group L with a controlled second-half performance against Panama, Bellingham and Kane striking within five minutes of each other to seal a clean-sheet 2-0 win that announced their intent without breaking sweat. DR Congo await in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 09:00 PT, a tie England enter as firm favourites — a competent but manageable assignment, provided the same composure carries over. Win that, and the draw opens enticingly: Ecuador or Mexico loom in the last 16, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Brazil or Norway that would constitute a genuine statement occasion either way.

France.text: France dismantled Sweden 3-0 in the Round of 32 with a performance that brooked no argument — Mbappé struck either side of half-time and Barcola added a second-half flourish, the clean sheet underlining a dominant group winners' authority. Paraguay await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, a tie France enter as heavy favourites; the South Americans will defend deep and make it scrappy, but the gulf in individual quality is steep. Should they advance past the quarterfinal — where Canada or Morocco lurk — a potential semifinal collision with Spain or Portugal would constitute the tournament's first genuine examination of their credentials.

Germany.text: Germany's Round of 32 exit is as brutal as the format can produce: Havertz's second-half equaliser cancelled out Enciso's opener, and when neither side could be separated across 90 minutes, Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four of four while Germany stumbled to three. A group-stage winner undone before the last sixteen, Joachim Löw's side — or whoever currently stewards this generation — departs a tournament they entered with genuine ambitions, beaten not by a superior team across the pitch but by the cold lottery of penalties.

Ghana.text: Ghana squeezed through Group L in third place, though their final group outing against Croatia offered little comfort — a 73rd-minute reply from Luckassen briefly threatened a point before Vlasic settled it for the Croatians ten minutes from time. Colombia await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a tie that favors the South Americans but is hardly a foregone conclusion given Ghana's capacity to land a punch. Should they advance, Algeria or Switzerland loom in the Round of 16, with Argentina a possible quarterfinal obstacle beyond that.

Haiti.text: Haiti depart the 2026 World Cup without a point to their name, their group stage exit confirmed by a 4-2 defeat to Morocco that was comfortable for the Atlas Lions despite a spirited Haitian showing either side of half-time. Two goals inside the first 43 minutes gave Haiti brief cause for optimism, but Morocco reasserted themselves emphatically in the closing stages, scoring four times in total to send their opponents home. The Caribbeans finish bottom of Group C, undone by a gulf in quality that proved too wide to bridge across three matches.

Iran.text: Iran's group-stage finale against Egypt ended 1-1, a Rezaeian equaliser on 14 minutes cancelling out Saber's early opener — a pattern that defined their entire tournament: always in the contest, never quite through it. Three draws, no wins, and three points were enough to sneak through as one of the best third-placed sides, but the knockout round delivered the reckoning that the group stage had deferred. Iran exit the 2026 World Cup without a victory to their name, their campaign a study in stubborn resilience that ultimately fell short of the cutting edge a deep run demands.

Iraq.text: Iraq's group stage ended in humiliation against Senegal, a 5-0 defeat that was effectively settled inside the opening minutes and then ruthlessly extended across the second half, with Gueye alone scoring twice in a 12-minute spell. Finishing fourth in Group I with three losses, no points, and twelve goals conceded, Iraq's tournament was over at the earliest stage — a chastening introduction to a World Cup where the gulf in class between the continent's elite and the tournament's newcomers was laid painfully bare.

Ivory Coast.text: Ivory Coast gave Norway a genuine contest in the Round of 32, pulling level through Diallo on 74 minutes after falling behind to Nusa's first-half opener — only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes remaining, a late hammer blow that extinguished the comeback. The Elephants depart a tournament they had navigated with enough promise to suggest they belonged, yet ultimately could not withstand Norway's cutting edge when it mattered most.

Japan.text: Japan held their own against Brazil for the better part of an hour, Sano's 29th-minute opener giving them genuine belief before Casemiro levelled midway through the second half. It was Martinelli's stoppage-time winner that delivered the cruellest of exits — a Samurai Blue side that had navigated Group F without defeat ultimately undone in the final seconds of a Round of 32 tie they had come agonisingly close to taking further.

Jordan.text: Jordan's group stage ended with a 3-1 defeat to Argentina — a result that followed the script almost from the first whistle, with the South Americans establishing a two-goal cushion inside half an hour before Messi put the contest beyond doubt late on. Al-Tamari's consolation just after the hour offered a brief flicker, but Jordan were outclassed. Finishing fourth in Group J with three defeats and no points, they nevertheless advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides before their World Cup came to a close.

Mexico.text: Mexico swept through Group A without conceding a goal, and their 3-0 dismissal of Czech Republic — all three strikes arriving after the hour, the last a stoppage-time flourish — carried the comfortable authority of a side that never needed to shift out of second gear. Ecuador await in the Round of 32 on Tue Jun 30 18:00 PT, a genuinely competitive South American side capable of disrupting rhythm, but El Tri's clean-sheet record and attacking depth make them clear favourites to advance. Survive that, and either England or DR Congo loom in the Round of 16, with a potential quarterfinal collision with Brazil adding serious weight to the latter stages of Mexico's draw.

Morocco.text: Morocco survived the most dramatic of exits from their Round of 32 tie against the Netherlands — trailing to Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike, they hauled themselves level through Diop deep into stoppage time and then held their nerve to win 3-2 on penalties. Canada await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, a fixture Morocco enter as clear favourites: the Atlas Lions' resilience and technical quality represent a significant step up from anything the Canadians have encountered. Should they advance, France or Paraguay could stand in their path at the quarterfinal stage — the prospect of a rematch with Les Bleus would constitute a genuine examination of Morocco's continental ambitions.

Netherlands.text: Netherlands dominated their group with seven points and a healthy goal difference, only to suffer one of the tournament's crueller exits — Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike cancelled out by a Moroccan equaliser deep in stoppage time, before the Dutch lost the subsequent shootout 2-3. It was the worst kind of departure: a lead surrendered at the death, then the lottery of penalties delivering the final blow. A side that had looked like genuine contenders through the group stage goes home from the Round of 32, undone in the space of twenty minutes.

New Zealand.text: Belgium administered a thorough lesson in the final group game, with Trossard's brace and further goals from De Bruyne and Lukaku turning what might have been a contest into a rout — New Zealand's consolation arriving far too late, in the 84th minute, to suggest any real foothold. The All Whites finished fourth in Group G with a single point and a goals difference of minus ten, their tournament record a frank reflection of the gulf between them and the competition's established forces. They depart a World Cup that offered little comfort beyond the experience itself.

Norway.text: Norway survived a nervy Round of 32 against Ivory Coast, Haaland's 86th-minute strike rescuing a result that had looked increasingly fragile after Diallo drew the Africans level just past the hour. That late intervention earned them a Round of 16 date with Brazil on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT — a forbidding assignment against one of the tournament's heavyweights, with Norway cast firmly as underdogs despite their striker's habit of settling tight matches. Should they find a way through, England or Mexico could await in the quarterfinal, with Argentina or Colombia lurking as potential semifinal obstacles on the road to the final.

Panama.text: Panama's group stage exit was as comprehensive as the record suggests — three defeats, no goals scored, and a closing loss to England that was settled efficiently in a five-minute spell midway through the second half, Bellingham and Kane putting the game to bed without drama or late anxiety. They finish bottom of Group L with nothing to show from their three outings, and whatever hope they carried into the tournament evaporated long before the knockout rounds began. A familiar story for Central American football at this level: the gap between qualifying and competing proved too wide to bridge.

Paraguay.text: Paraguay's passage through the Round of 32 was the stuff of genuine drama — Enciso's first-half strike answered by Havertz just after the interval, then ninety minutes of nerve shredding its way to a penalty shootout that Paraguay edged 4-3 to eliminate Germany. Now they meet France in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, and few neutrals will give them much hope: this is as steep a climb as the draw can produce, with Paraguay arriving as heavy underdogs against one of the tournament's genuine contenders. Survive that, and Canada or Morocco await in the quarterfinal — a considerably more navigable prospect where Paraguay's resilience could count for something real.

Portugal.text: Portugal's goalless draw with Colombia to close the group stage was functional rather than alarming — a second-place finish secured, the work largely done in earlier rounds. They face Croatia in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT, a fixture that carries genuine weight: these two have history, Croatia are never easily dismissed, and Portugal, despite their six-goal advantage across the group, carry an unresolved question about sharpness in the big moments. Win that and a Round of 16 collision with Spain looms as the most electrifying possibility, with France a potential semifinal opponent to sharpen the mind still further.

Qatar.text: Qatar's group stage exit arrived with little ceremony — a 1-3 defeat to Bosnia & Herzegovina in which they trailed by two goals before half-time, Al-Haydos pulling one back on the stroke of the interval before Mahmic's 80th-minute strike killed any lingering hope of a comeback. The hosts finished bottom of Group B with a single point and a goal difference of minus eight, their tournament a sobering exercise in the gap between ambition and execution at this level.

Saudi Arabia.text: Saudi Arabia's group stage ended with a goalless draw against Cape Verde — a stalemate that was as flat as the scoreline suggests, neither side able to manufacture a breakthrough across ninety minutes. That result, combined with a goal difference of minus five across three matches, proved just enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, but the Green Falcons were eliminated in the Round of 32, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Scotland.text: Brazil ended Scotland's tournament before it had truly begun, a 3-0 defeat that was every bit as one-sided as the scoreline suggests. Júnior struck twice in the first half — the second arriving deep in added time — before Cunha added a third shortly after the interval, leaving Scotland with nothing to build on. They depart having advanced from the group stage as one of the best third-placed sides, a modest distinction that flatters a campaign yielding just one win and four goals conceded. The 2026 World Cup ends for Scotland at the Round of 32.

Senegal.text: Senegal closed the group stage with a statement: a 5-0 dismantling of Iraq that flattered nobody, the goals spread across both halves and Gueye helping himself to a brace as the Lions of Teranga finished on the right side of the ledger after a turbulent campaign. That result secured passage as one of the best third-placed teams, but the reward is a Round of 32 assignment against Belgium on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT — a genuine test, with the Belgians carrying the weight of expectation and Senegal needing to reproduce that clinical edge to cause an upset. Survive that, and Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinal, a reminder of just how steep the road gets from here.

South Africa.text: South Africa's World Cup ended in the cruelest fashion, a 90th-minute goal from Eustáquio giving Canada a 1-0 win that the balance of the match may not have fully demanded. Bafana Bafana had navigated the group stage with enough grit to finish second in Group A, but a tournament played on home soil — or at least in their confederate hemisphere — ultimately closed in the dying seconds of a Round of 32 tie they very nearly survived.

South Korea.text: South Korea close their World Cup 2026 account with a flat 0-1 defeat to South Africa, Maseko's goal just past the hour mark enough to settle a match the Taeguk Warriors never truly threatened to turn. They had squeezed through the group phase as one of the best third-placed sides, but that slender lifeline ran out in the round of 32 — eliminated without a goal scored in their final two outings, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Spain.text: Spain closed out Group H with a controlled, professional win over Uruguay — Baena's first-half strike the only goal in a clean sheet that confirmed their status as group winners without ever requiring a second gear. Austria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT, a manageable assignment on paper for a side of Spain's pedigree, though Austrian football has grown increasingly awkward to play against. Should they progress, Croatia or Portugal — both familiar, combustible rivals — could arrive in the Round of 16, and France or Morocco lurk as potential semifinal opponents capable of making this a genuine examination.

Sweden.text: Sweden's tournament ends with a 0-3 defeat to France in the Round of 32 — a verdict that was effectively delivered at half-time, Mbappé's first strike just before the break puncturing whatever hope the Swedes had carried into the tie, and a Barcola goal eight minutes into the second half rendering the contest settled long before his second. They had advanced from Group F as one of the better third-placed sides, a narrow path that always pointed toward exactly this kind of reckoning: a French side operating at a different altitude. The margin was honest.

Switzerland.text: Switzerland topped Group B in purposeful fashion, turning in a composed second-half performance against Canada — Vargas and Manzambi striking either side of the hour to put the Swiss firmly in control before David's late consolation added a touch of nerves to an otherwise authoritative 2-1 win. Algeria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a tie Switzerland will enter as clear favourites: the Swiss carry genuine tournament pedigree while Algeria, though capable of disruption, represent a manageable assignment for a side in this form. Survive that, and a quarterfinal collision with Argentina looms as a distinct possibility — a far sterner examination of whether Switzerland's solidity can withstand elite pressure.

Tunisia.text: Tunisia's group stage exit was written early: two Dutch goals inside seven minutes buried the Carthage Eagles before the match had found its shape, and though Mastouri pulled one back after the break, Hecke's strike shortly after the hour restored the three-goal cushion and sealed a 3-1 defeat. Tunisia finished bottom of Group F without a point, their record of three defeats and a goal difference of minus ten telling the story of a tournament in which they were consistently outclassed — a dispiriting campaign ended against one of the competition's heavyweights.

Turkey.text: Turkey's tournament ends in the cruelest of fashions — a 3-2 win over the United States that arrived too late to matter. They trailed inside three minutes, fought back to lead through Güler and Yilmaz, conceded again early in the second half, and only sealed it through Ayhan's injury-time strike. The drama was real, the effort undeniable, but a fourth-place group finish — even as one of the better third-placed sides — proved an insufficient foundation, and Turkey's World Cup 2026 is over at the group stage.

USA.text: USA topped Group D despite a bruising final group-stage defeat to Turkey, conceding an 98th-minute winner to leave on a sour note after Trusty's early opener and a Berhalter goal just after the break had briefly suggested parity. The hosts now face Bosnia & Herzegovina in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT — a fixture that favors the Americans, though Bosnia are capable of disrupting a side whose defensive frailties Turkey so ruthlessly exposed. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal await in the Round of 16, with the quarterfinal potentially producing a heavyweight collision against Portugal or Spain.

Uruguay.text: Uruguay's group stage ended with a narrow 0-1 defeat to Spain, Álex Baena's first-half strike the solitary difference in a contest that offered the Uruguayans little joy in the final third. Despite advancing as one of the best third-placed sides, their tournament came to an abrupt halt at the same juncture — two draws and a loss across Group H, with a negative goal difference, telling a story of a side that never quite found its footing at this World Cup.

Uzbekistan.text: Uzbekistan's group stage ended with a 1-3 defeat to DR Congo, a result that traced a familiar arc: an encouraging early lead through Shomurodov, then a second-half capitulation as Wissa and Mayele dismantled them after the hour mark, Wissa adding a third in stoppage time to put a harsh gloss on the scoreline. The Central Asians finished bottom of Group K without a point, conceding eleven goals across three matches, and no late reprieve arrived. Their debut on the expanded World Cup stage was brief and bruising, a tournament that exposed the gulf between ambition and readiness at the highest level.
